title: Tolpis succulenta
intro: Tolpis succulenta, commonly known as Succulent Tolpis, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Tolpis succulenta include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tolpis succulenta is traditionally used in herbal medicine, often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, to treat various ailments, although its efficacy and safety are still being researched and verified.
taxonomy: Tolpis succulenta is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The genus Tolpis is a group of succulent plants that are primarily found in the Mediterranean region. Within the Asteraceae family, Tolpis succulenta is placed in the subfamily Cichorioideae. The subfamily Cichorioideae is further divided into several tribes, with Tolpis succulenta belonging to the tribe Cichorieae. Tolpis succulenta is a member of the plant kingdom, kingdom Plantae, and is also classified as an angiosperm, a flowering plant that produces seeds within a fruit.
